Package microui.component

Class Knob

java.lang.Object
microui.core.base.View
microui.core.base.SpatialView
microui.core.base.ContentView
microui.core.base.Component
microui.core.RangeControl
microui.component.Knob
All Implemented Interfaces:
Scrollable, ValuePreviewSource, Visible
public final class Knob extends RangeControl
A graphical knob component that allows users to select a value from a range by rotating a handle along a circular scale.

The knob supports mouse/touch dragging, mouse wheel scrolling, and double-click to reset to a default value. Appearance properties such as colors, handle size/offset, scale thickness, and start/end angles are fully customizable.

See Also:

  • Constructor Details

    • Knob

      public Knob(float x, float y, float width, float height)
      Constructs a Knob with the specified bounds.
      Parameters:
      x - the x-coordinate of the knob
      y - the y-coordinate of the knob
      width - the width of the knob
      height - the height of the knob

    • Knob

      public Knob()
      Constructs a Knob with default size, centered on the screen.

  • Method Details

    • getScaleColor

      public AbstractColor getScaleColor()
      Returns the color used for the scale arc.
      Returns:
      the scale color

    • setScaleColor

      public void setScaleColor(AbstractColor scaleColor)
      Sets the color of the scale arc.
      Parameters:
      scaleColor - the new scale color (must not be null)
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if scaleColor is null

    • setScaleColor

      public void setScaleColor(AbstractColor startColor, AbstractColor endColor)
      Sets the scale color using an interpolated gradient between two colors. The interpolation factor is derived from the current value's position within the min/max range.
      Parameters:
      startColor - the color used at the minimum value
      endColor - the color used at the maximum value

    • getHandleOffsetRatio

      public float getHandleOffsetRatio()
      Returns the ratio that determines how far from the center the handle is drawn. A value of 0.0f places the handle at the center; 0.4000000059604645f places it near the edge.
      Returns:
      the handle offset ratio (between 0.0f and 0.4000000059604645f)

    • setHandleOffsetRatio

      public void setHandleOffsetRatio(float handleOffsetRatio)
      Sets the handle offset ratio. Controls the distance of the handle from the knob's center.
      Parameters:
      handleOffsetRatio - the new ratio (must be between 0.0f and 0.4000000059604645f)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the value is out of allowed bounds

    • getHandleSizeRatio

      public float getHandleSizeRatio()
      Returns the ratio of the handle size relative to the knob's diameter.
      Returns:
      the handle size ratio (between 0.10000000149011612f and 1.0f)

    • setHandleSizeRatio

      public void setHandleSizeRatio(float handleSizeRatio)
      Sets the handle size ratio. Determines how large the handle circle is compared to the overall knob size.
      Parameters:
      handleSizeRatio - the new ratio (must be between 0.10000000149011612f and 1.0f)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the value is out of allowed bounds

    • getDefaultValue

      public Float getDefaultValue()
      Returns the default value to which the knob resets on double-click.
      Returns:
      the default value, or null if not set

    • setDefaultValue

      public void setDefaultValue(float defaultValue)
      Sets the default value for the knob. When the knob is double-clicked, the current value is reset to this default.
      Parameters:
      defaultValue - the default value (must be within the current min/max range)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if defaultValue is outside the valid range

    • getScaleWeightRatio

      public float getScaleWeightRatio()
      Returns the scale weight ratio, which controls the thickness of the scale arc.
      Returns:
      the scale weight ratio (between 0.0f and 2.0f)

    • setScaleWeightRatio

      public void setScaleWeightRatio(float scaleWeightRatio)
      Sets the scale weight ratio. Higher values produce a thicker scale arc.
      Parameters:
      scaleWeightRatio - the new ratio (must be between 0.0f and 2.0f)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the ratio is out of bounds

    • getHandleColor

      public AbstractColor getHandleColor()
      Returns the color used for the handle.
      Returns:
      the handle color

    • setHandleColor

      public void setHandleColor(AbstractColor handleColor)
      Sets the color of the handle.
      Parameters:
      handleColor - the new handle color (must not be null)
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if handleColor is null

    • getStartAngle

      public float getStartAngle()
      Returns the start angle of the scale arc, in radians.
      Returns:
      the start angle (0 вЂ¦ 2ПЂ)

    • setStartAngle

      public void setStartAngle(float startAngle)
      Sets the start angle of the scale arc.
      Parameters:
      startAngle - the start angle in radians (must be between 0 and 2ПЂ, and not greater than the current end angle)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the angle is invalid or exceeds the end angle

    • getEndAngle

      public float getEndAngle()
      Returns the end angle of the scale arc, in radians.
      Returns:
      the end angle (0 вЂ¦ 2ПЂ)

    • setEndAngle

      public void setEndAngle(float endAngle)
      Sets the end angle of the scale arc.
      Parameters:
      endAngle - the end angle in radians (must be between 0 and 2ПЂ, and not lower than the current start angle)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the angle is invalid or below the start angle

    • setAngles

      public void setAngles(float startAngle, float endAngle)
      Convenience method to set both start and end angles simultaneously.
      Parameters:
      startAngle - the start angle in radians (0 вЂ¦ 2ПЂ)
      endAngle - the end angle in radians (0 вЂ¦ 2ПЂ, must be greater than startAngle)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if either angle is invalid or if startAngle >= endAngle

    • isContentPrepared

      public boolean isContentPrepared()
      Determines whether the knob is ready to receive input. The knob is considered prepared if the mouse is inside its circular area, or if it is being dragged, or if internal scrolling is active.

      On Android, the knob does not respond to a simple press without movement; the mouse must be inside the circle and the component must be pressed. If another component already owns the pointer, this knob will not react.

      Returns:
      true if the knob should handle input, false otherwise

    • mouseWheel

      public void mouseWheel(processing.event.MouseEvent mouseEvent)
      Handles mouse wheel events. If the mouse is inside the knob, scrolling is initiated.
      Parameters:
      mouseEvent - the mouse wheel event
